This plant is a member of the Annonaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. intro: Pseuduvaria parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseuduvaria parviflora include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to possess cytotoxic and antitumor activities. Pseuduvaria parviflora is typically used to make various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
